title: T-055 — Factory release and D365 posture guard for scheduler inputs
prompt:
# T-055 — Factory release and D365 posture guard for scheduler inputs

PRD: `07-PLANNING-EXT-PRD.md` §5.5 input data, §15 dependencies, Wave0 factory release decisions.
UX/prototype refs are listed in `pipeline_inputs.scope_files`.
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
Harden 07-EXT against release-model drift before solver execution.

## Implementation contract
The scheduler input loader must read the same canonical factory release read model used by 04/08 and return explicit blockers for non-usable WOs. No D365 path can unlock factory scheduling.

ACP shape reminder: keep this task as one atomic implementation. Do not add ACP-owned fields. Keep local dependencies in `dependencies`; cross-module contracts in `cross_module_dependencies` are blockers/inputs, not ACP local task IDs.

pipeline_inputs.description: Ensure scheduler input loader consumes only Planning WOs whose canonical factory release snapshot is factory-usable; D365 metadata never unlocks scheduling.
pipeline_inputs.details: 07-EXT is not the release owner. It reads Planning/Technical canonical active_bom_header_id and active_factory_spec_id status and excludes/blocks WOs that are not approved_for_factory or released_to_factory. This aligns with 04/08 Wave0 release model.

cross_module_dependencies.reason: Planning snapshots active BOM/spec IDs onto WOs.
Production runtime uses the same factory-usable status matrix.

acceptance_criteria: Given WO canonical status is approved_for_factory or released_to_factory and snapshot IDs match, then it can enter scheduler input.
Given status is pending/blocked/missing/mismatched, then it is excluded or returned with typed release_blocker and no assignment is created.
Given D365 Built/export/preload/sync metadata exists, then it does not make a non-factory-usable WO schedulable.
test_strategy: RED unit tests for status matrix, missing/mismatched active IDs, D365 no-op and success cases.
risk_red_lines: Do not invent a 07-owned release enum.
Do not infer release from D365 or bom_released flags.
Do not select latest BOM/spec at scheduling time.
pipeline_inputs.priority_normalization_note: Priority normalized to ACP convention bands on 2026-05-03; lower number means earlier execution.
